summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the vulnerable upload-user-file endpoint writes attacker-controlled content to an arbitrary filesystem path outside the intended user-files directory when the x-actual-file-id header contains path traversal sequences.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the server to write a file containing a known proof string to /tmp/evohunt-traversal-proof.blob, which is outside the /data/user-files/ directory, demonstrating arbitrary file write via path traversal.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched the Actual Budget sync-server from the pinned Docker image, bootstrapped the server with a password, authenticated, uploaded a legitimate control file to /data/user-files/ (confirming normal operation), then uploaded a file with a crafted x-actual-file-id header containing /../../../tmp/evohunt-traversal-proof. The proof file was found at /tmp/evohunt-traversal-proof.blob containing the attacker-controlled proof payload.
verification_method: The exploit script uploads a control file first (verifying normal upload works and the file lands in /data/user-files/), then uploads the traversal payload. It checks whether the file exists at the traversed path (/tmp/evohunt-traversal-proof.blob) and verifies the file content contains the expected proof string. This distinguishes a real path traversal from a mere upload error or unrelated file.
evidence_produced_by_run: The control file was correctly written to /data/user-files/file-control-file-id.blob, confirming the server handles normal uploads as expected.
A file containing the proof string EVOHUNT-TRaversal-Proof-Of-Concept-Payload was found at /tmp/evohunt-traversal-proof.blob, which is outside the /data/user-files/ directory.
The traversal file content matches the attacker-controlled request body, confirming the path traversal wrote the payload to the unintended location.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable tag, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
